atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports left shoulder and elbow pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain in his left shoulder and left elbow for approximately five months.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the pain began five months ago after falling down a flight of icy stairs at home.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports landing on his left side during the fall.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports continuing to lift weights at the gym twice a week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports reducing the weight due to the pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the pain is worse when lifting.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports stopping lifting more than 15 or 20 pounds.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports walking approximately 30 minutes almost every day with his wife.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any numbness or tingling.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports trying icing the area.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies taking any pain medication.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports constant and frustrating pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation over the left shoulder and elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Hawkins-Kennedy and Neer's tests are positive.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Proximal and distal upper extremity strength is intact.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Median and radial-ulnar sensation are intact.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Abduction is limited to 90 degrees.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Empty can test is normal.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's presentation is most consistent with impingement syndrome of the left shoulder.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The history of the fall and subsequent pain is concerning for a possible rotator cuff injury.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A referral has been requested for Dr. Martha Rivera to provide physical therapy two days per week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient will start physical therapy and continue icing the area and resting.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A cortisone injection will be considered if physical therapy does not improve symptoms.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient should call or email us if symptoms worsen or do not improve with therapy.
